reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that for $\alpha \in S_n$ and any $i$, "$\alpha$ moves $i$" iff "$\alpha^{-1}$ moves $i$". The Lean statement encodes "moves $i$" correctly as `α i ≠ i`, and uses `Equiv.Perm u` (Mathlib's type of permutations of `u`) with `α⁻¹` for the inverse permutation. The only notable difference is that Lean generalizes the ambient type `u` from a finite set (as $S_n$ literally means symmetric group on $n$ elements) to any type. However, the property "permutation moves $i$ iff inverse permutation moves $i$" is a purely formal property of bijections that holds identically in any setting, with the same proof. This is the natural Mathlib-style formulation of the same mathematical content and would be accepted as faithfully capturing the informal claim under standard mathematical reading; the finiteness restriction plays no role in the statement's meaning.
